title: Ophrys ustulata
taxonomy: Ophrys ustulata is a species of orchid in the genus Ophrys. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which comprises approximately 30,000 species of orchids. As a member of the genus Ophrys, Ophrys ustulata is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The genus Ophrys is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its bee orchids, which mimic the appearance and scent of female bees to attract pollinators. Ophrys ustulata is a rare and endemic species found in the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa.
